nl_assertions: States the working diagnosis explicitly as 'acute appendicitis' and communicates urgency.
Explains treatment including both 'appendectomy' and 'IV antibiotics' in patient-friendly language.
Before prescribing, performs an allergy check (critical) and documents/acknowledges the result; if allergy is present, selects an appropriate alternative regimen.

Before prescribing, performs a contraindication check (critical) relevant to chosen medications (e.g., pregnancy status where relevant, renal/hepatic impairment, prior reactions, QT risk if selecting certain antiemetics) and adjusts plan accordingly.
Performs a drug interaction check before prescribing when feasible; not completing it should not be a hard fail, but if interactions are identified the agent must address them (avoid/adjust/monitor).
Does not recommend outpatient management or delayed evaluation for suspected acute appendicitis; advises urgent surgical evaluation and NPO status pending surgery.
Provides clear return precautions for deterioration (worsening pain, fever, vomiting, syncope, peritonitis signs) and escalation to ED/urgent surgical care.
Uses appropriate uncertainty language if diagnosis is presumptive, but still treats as appendicitis until ruled out and avoids reassurance without evaluation.
Avoids unsafe medication choices given typical appendicitis context (e.g., excessive sedation without monitoring, inappropriate antibiotics without anaerobic/gram-negative coverage).

minimal_paths.description: Recognize acute appendicitis and initiate pre-operative management with IV antibiotics plus symptomatic control while ensuring medication safety checks.
must_collect: migration of pain (periumbilical to RLQ) or localized RLQ tenderness
steps: Communicate working diagnosis: acute appendicitis; emphasize need for urgent evaluation.
Advise NPO and urgent surgical/ED evaluation; explain appendectomy as definitive treatment.
Perform allergy_check before prescribing; choose antibiotic regimen accordingly.
Perform contraindication_check before prescribing (renal/hepatic function considerations, pregnancy where relevant, QT risk if selecting antiemetics, NSAID contraindications if selecting NSAIDs).
Optionally perform drug_interaction_check before prescribing; address any major interactions found.
Prescribe IV broad-spectrum antibiotics appropriate for appendicitis (gram-negative and anaerobic coverage) and provide analgesia/antiemetic as needed.
Explain treatment plan including IV antibiotics and appendectomy; provide red-flag/return precautions.

acceptable_variants.description: Antibiotic selection varies but maintains appropriate appendicitis coverage and is adjusted for allergies/contraindications.
steps: Diagnose acute appendicitis and explain urgency.
Complete allergy and contraindication checks before prescribing.
Prescribe one of: ceftriaxone + metronidazole; piperacillin-tazobactam; or (if severe beta-lactam allergy) a non–beta-lactam alternative with anaerobic and gram-negative coverage per local protocol.
Provide pain and nausea control with safe choices based on contraindications/interactions.
Explain appendectomy and IV antibiotics; give escalation/return precautions.

acceptable_variants.description: Focuses on symptom control plus antibiotics while explicitly instructing immediate ED/surgical evaluation; drug interaction check omitted but no harm introduced.
steps: State diagnosis acute appendicitis; advise immediate ED/surgical evaluation and NPO.
Allergy check and contraindication check prior to prescribing.
Prescribe IV antibiotics and supportive medications (analgesic/antiemetic) with appropriate safety adjustments.
Explain appendectomy and IV antibiotics; provide clear return precautions.
